title: verbascum × lychnitis nothosubsp. nuriense
intro: Verbascum × lychnitis nothosubsp. nuriense is a rare, nothosubspecies of the Verbascum lychnitis species, commonly known as the mullein. This biennial plant is native to South Korea and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The roots and leaves of Verbascum × lychnitis nothosubsp. nuriense contain mucilage, saponins, and iridoids, which are known to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, providing health benefits such as soothing sore throats, calming coughs, and aiding in wound healing. In traditional herbal medicine, Verbascum × lychnitis nothosubsp. nuriense is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
